analysis_result: 50% (4/8) of MPNST-G1 tumors show 20p loss. Samples with 20p loss: M1014 (log2 ratio -0.55), M6712 (-0.28), M803 (-0.25), M2346 (-0.19). Samples without 20p loss: M3048 (-0.02), M2372 (+0.03), M3398 (+0.07), M2252 (+0.07). Using a more stringent threshold (log2<-0.2), the frequency is 37.5% (3/8).
denominator_used: N=8; All MPNST-G1 samples per Methylation_Group column in Supplementary Data 1 (MOESM4); 100% of MPNST_G1 tumors had methylation IDAT data available.
assumptions_made: CONUMEE R/Bioconductor package was not installed, so used an equivalent Python homemade pipeline: (1) parsed raw IDAT files with methylprep to obtain total probe intensity (Meth+Unmeth) on autosomes; (2) log2-transformed sample intensities and centered each sample by its median autosomal intensity; (3) used the 82 benign/premalignant NF samples (Benign_NF_G4, PremalignantNF_G3, Cutaneous_NF_G6, Cutaneous_NF_G7) as a CONUMEE-style reference panel (median log2 ratio per probe); (4) computed per-sample log2 ratio = sample_log_int minus reference_log_int at each probe; (5) assigned probes to chromosome 20p (MAPINFO < 26,369,569 hg19) or 20q (MAPINFO > 28,265,449 hg19) using manifest coordinates, excluding the centromeric region; (6) took the median log2 ratio across all 7,188 20p probes per sample; (7) called 20p loss as sample 20p-median log2 < -0.1 (standard CONUMEE arm-level threshold, ~6 SD below reference). The reference-sample 20p distribution was very tight (median 0.000, SD 0.017, range -0.04 to +0.07), so -0.1 is a conservative cutoff. Specificity check: 20p loss calls were 0/92 in all non-MPNST_G1 groups vs 4/8 in MPNST_G1, supporting the calls as real biological events rather than technical noise.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
- Clinical data: 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx 'Clinical Data' sheet, 108 samples x 15 columns
- Raw IDATs: GSE207207_idats/ - 108 Grn+Red IDAT pairs (216 files) + MethylationEPIC manifest
- Manifest: GPL21145_MethylationEPIC_15073387_v-1-0.csv.gz, 868,565 probes (848,166 on autosomes with valid chr/position)

B. COHORT DEFINITION
- All samples with Methylation_Group=='MPNST_G1' -> 8 samples
  (M1014, M2252, M2346, M2372, M3048, M3398, M6712, M803)
- All 8 have matching Sentrix_ID in IDAT files (100% methylation data availability)
- Reference panel: 82 non-MPNST samples (Benign_NF-G4 n=20, PremalignantNF_G3 n=29, Cutaneous_NF_G6 n=12, Cutaneous_NF_G7 n=21)

C. VARIABLE CODING
- Outcome: arm-level 20p loss = per-sample median log2 intensity ratio on 20p probes < -0.1
- CONUMEE R/Bioconductor package is not installed in this environment. Used equivalent Python pipeline via methylprep.

D. COMPUTATION
- Parsed 108 IDAT pairs; for each probe computed total fluorescence:
    Type II probes: Total = Grn[AddrA] + Red[AddrA]
    Type I Red probes: Total = Red[AddrA] + Red[AddrB]
    Type I Grn probes: Total = Grn[AddrA] + Grn[AddrB]
  Yielded 846,232 autosomal probes x 108 samples after dropping NaN addresses.
- Log2(total_intensity), centered each sample by its autosomal median.
- Reference probe-level log2 = median across 82 reference samples.
- Log2 ratio per probe per sample = sample_log - reference_log.
- 20p region (hg19, excluding centromere 26,369,569-28,265,449): 7,188 probes.
- Per-sample 20p arm median log2 ratio computed.

E. RESULT
- Reference samples (n=82) 20p log2 distribution: median 0.000, SD 0.017, range [-0.04, +0.07].
- MPNST_G1 samples (n=8) 20p median log2 ratio:
    M1014:  -0.555  <- LOSS
    M6712:  -0.282  <- LOSS
    M803:   -0.251  <- LOSS
    M2346:  -0.186  <- LOSS
    M3048:  -0.020
    M2372:  +0.034
    M3398:  +0.072
    M2252:  +0.072
- Threshold log2<-0.1: 4/8 MPNST_G1 tumors (50%) have 20p loss.
- Threshold log2<-0.15: 4/8 (50%) - same four samples.
- Threshold log2<-0.2: 3/8 (37.5%).
- Cross-group specificity at log2<-0.1: 
    MPNST_G1: 4/8 (50%)
    MPNST_G2: 0/8 (0%)
    All NF groups: 0/92 (0%)
- All four MPNST_G1 samples with 20p loss show preserved 20q (log2 near 0 or slightly positive), consistent with true 20p-arm-specific loss, not whole-chromosome-20 loss or sample-wide bias.

F. CAVEATS
- True CONUMEE (R/Bioconductor) was not available; the Python pipeline emulates its core logic (total-intensity normalization + reference panel + arm-level median log2), but omits CONUMEE's fitted CNV segmentation, detail-region annotation, and noise-model-based significance testing. Rigorous CONUMEE segmentation with circular binary segmentation might classify slightly differently at borderline samples.
- The reference panel is benign/premalignant NF tumors rather than healthy non-neoplastic tissue (paper-provided data does not contain non-neoplastic reference samples); benign NF tumors are near-diploid and a reasonable surrogate, and indeed showed a very narrow 20p log2 distribution.
- Threshold of -0.1 is the standard CONUMEE-like arm-level call cutoff but is a heuristic; at -0.15 the same 4 samples call loss, supporting robustness. At the stringent -0.2 threshold one sample (M2346 at -0.19) drops out, giving 3/8 = 37.5%.
- Sample-level CNV from methylation arrays is less precise than WES- or array-CGH-based CNV; borderline calls (e.g. sample M2346 at -0.19) might be reclassified with independent copy-number data.
- X/Y chromosomes were excluded from the analysis (standard for CNV pipelines without sex matching); chr 20 is autosomal so this is not relevant.
- Assumption: hg19 coordinates in the EPIC manifest match the paper's reference build. (The manifest header lists Genome_Build 37.)

Primary answer: 50% (4/8) of MPNST-G1 tumors have 20p loss by CONUMEE-equivalent methylation CNV analysis; an alternative stringent threshold gives 37.5% (3/8).
